raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I purchased a flight through the XXXX website. Less than XXXX hour after receiving the flight confirmation email, I received an email from XXXX stating that the flight and my transaction were being canceled due to suspected fraud. 

After XXXX weeks without receiving a refund, I contacted XXXX and requested that they process the refund. After an additional month passed without receiving the refund, I initiated a dispute with Citibank in XX/XX/XXXX. 

As part of the dispute, I submitted my timestamped flight confirmation email as well as the timestamped email from XXXX confirming that the ticket had been canceled due to suspected fraud. 

During Citibanks review of the dispute, they relied on documentation that clearly belonged to a different customer. The documentation referenced a {$99.00} transaction for a golf membership belonging to a man named XXXX XXXX in XXXX I was provided with XXXX XXXX personal information, including his phone number, email address, home address, credit card number, and golf membership account details. This represents a significant breach of sensitive personal information. Furthermore, this documentation is entirely unrelated to my dispute. 

Citibank did not appear to review any documentation from XXXX regarding my transaction. Instead, they stated that XXXX claimed that I received the service/product, which is impossible because the ticket was canceled within minutes of purchase by XXXX and XXXX airline. 

To date, Citibank has closed the dispute. I requested to speak with a supervisor and was told that the only option available is an internal audit and that the dispute can not be reopened. I was also offered a {$250.00} statement credit, which I will not accept. 

Because I still needed the flight, I was forced to purchase another ticket through a different website using a different credit card. As a result, I am currently at a loss of {$910.00}.
